verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (transmembrane emp24 domain-containing protein 10) is a component of the COPII vesicle coat machinery and resides in the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment (ERGIC) and cis-Golgi. It is an integral membrane protein of the endomembrane system with no documented mechanism to reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. While it is a multi-pass TM protein, its topology orients it for intra-Golgi trafficking, not surface display. No cell-state, tissue-restricted, or dual-localization variant surfaces it extracellularly. It is a cargo-selection and membrane-trafficking component, not a surface receptor or PM-resident protein.
